saying yeah, they go like this They're not go from a five to an eight slide back to a six go to a eight point five slide back to a seven Go there they go. It's you get it. It doesn't happen in one straight line You can't do it too much would have to be said you couldn't focus on both logic and emotion at the same time They're different things. There's no so if you try to do it all in one shot Then what you do is you'd simply have one you talk talk all the way Ask for the order and they buy Which is what most salesman don't know how to sell do they talk and they make the whole thing up front They front load and when they're done the person can never they you know, someone has us like we all have like a cap I can only go I can go from a five to a seven point five But not on what you say to me. I can't be made more certain until I catch my breath I need to catch my breath and think about things before I become any more certain You could pour on the kitchen sink, but not until I stop As you will question get to mow it through my mind again Take a breath considerate then I can be moved to the next plateau of certainty That's how human beings are built So understand the reason why the straight line is Constructed the way it's constructed with loops and stopping off points is because there's all these rules of human nature that Born-closers intuitively know and respond to but 99 point nancy the world doesn't intuitively know it So I've constructed a system that allows you to not break the laws of nature While you're going out and making your airtight logical cases You have to make two cases here an airtight logical case and airtight most this you gotta do with out breaking rapport Without pissing people off without them tuning out without overwhelming them Get it So that is a little is a lot going on you can't do it in one shot So I realized when I was very young when I watch people selling it didn't know what they were doing They just try to throw the whole kitchen sink at someone they never could close Never would close well. I Would say you know what I'll frame my case and Then wait for them to say let me think about it so they could catch their breath Say that sounds good. You're let me think about it And then I get a chance now to move to the next level of certainty And I also realized that wow I could do that five or six times They don't they don't hang up on me. They don't walk out the door They just keep listening as long as I stay in rapport and smile the right way and See there's all these rules that are all floating around and governing human activity and the straight line Operates with this universe of rules You don't have to know every rule every second while you're selling just to buy by the rules of the straight line I've done that for you Okay, now the deeper you understand. Yes, of course the better it is But for beginners and you just gonna still worry about it Just trust me until you understand all the rules Which is what the daily motivations about in the weekly thing I dig in the stuff And eventually you get all of it and it makes you that much better So the point is what is looping? The question that that that ticked me off not not me me not not me me I mean mainly realize This was the necessary training was that he's like when I loop do I talk about the first time and Then in the second loop I talk about the second ten and then I told what the he wasn't sure Fair enough whether you should run one loop for each of the three tens and of course you don't okay So let me explain now what you do when you loop looping is My solution to all those rules of human nature That you just simply have to abide by if you want to close the deal I mentioned the few before and there's others as well So what happens is since we know that we can't front load We can't just keep talking and talking and talking and try to get someone to an absolute ten both logically and emotionally Can't do it in one shot Instead we frame our case so the front half of the sale is about Extracting intelligence finding out what someone needs Building massive rapport taking control of the sale position ourselves as an expert and then beginning the process of framing that airtight logical and emotional Case and then we ask for the order for the first time now Chances are very very high unless it's a lay down That they're not gonna buy from you right? They're not certain enough yet Or they might have a very high action threshold or maybe in denial of their pain. There are these things happening So what we do the reason I called a loop is because theoretically When you close out the front half of the sale right here This is the end of the front half of the sale with a front side of the sale right? You've just finished making a sales Presentation a formal presentation. We say here's my product Let me explain to you how it works why it's awesome why it's gonna fill your needs why it's gonna resolve you explain that all in a way And then you end by asking for the order for the first time Right, that's a formal presentation a straight line presentation Right when you ask for the order for the first time What can happen? There's only three things that can happen. They can say Yes, in which case you close the deal to lay down no objections They could say no Which is very rare to say now I'm not interested because you've already filtered to know people out in intelligence gathering right or They could say maybe and Maybe represents that basket of common objections only think about like what your back bed tell me a blob all those common objections, right? So when someone gives you an objection like that, we know that what what is it? It's a smoke screen for uncertainty What type of answer is it about uncertainty about the product? Is it about you? Is it about your company? Or maybe they have a really high action threshold or they're just in denial of their pain Which one who fucking knows we're not mind readers. So looping What looping represents what it is is a very Sophisticated but simple to learn step-by-step approach to using the process of elimination And those I know that all three tens have to line up logically and emotionally and at some points got to exceed their action threshold and we could add on paying to lower their action Especially for that. I know that has that right so what I do is I say, okay? step one I must get them to attend for the product so I a loop begins by I loop back into Presentation mode I go back into another I have to make a follow-up Presentation that picks up where my initial presentation left off. I'm looping so watch I made my presentation, but guess what they're still not certain enough. So I have to loop back To the point where I left us I framed my case now I'm gonna make a secondary case that picks up where the first case left off and this case is gonna be even more powerful So watch if someone started a five and I got into a seven with my first case my goal of my second airtight My my my my my my my second presentation is to get now to maybe a nine nine point five I loop back and I have my first ten I want to really lock that first ten in because I know I couldn't go from five to ten in one shot It breaks too many rules of human of how human beings process information Logically and emotionally I got to do it in multiple shots. So my first shot I loop back and I Make my secondary presentation about the product and say you see what I'm saying now Does that make sense to the guy says oh, yeah, no now I get it In other words when I'm done with my secondary presentation for the product I think you see what I'm saying does that make sense now like I understand maybe I don't say I understand that you weren't You were to seven four, but now do you see what I'm saying Bill? And he says oh, yeah now I get I say exactly It really is a great situation. It's a great product. It fills your needs now. I could Then ask for the order again, but I don't want to risk that because here's why All I'll do here if I ask for the order with just creating massive certainty for the first ten My odds are very low That he's also high enough on the certain scale for the second and third ten and if one of them is too low a Not gonna be able to close I don't want to risk getting another Let me I don't want to ask for the order again because I'm all I'm gonna do is force him to do what hop to a different Objection he'll just change his objection until I get into a 10 10 10 and it exceeds his action threshold I'm just gonna get a new one say he'll go from let me think about saying oh, yeah, it sounds good Let me just call you back. He'll hop to a new objection. So rather than risking that happening right here I will then transition from so I said exactly see it now as it makes sense. So oh, yeah, no, it's great Exactly, let me ask you another question if I've been your broker or whatever you then run your Forest gun pattern and start talking about you. This is all part of one loop So mean you've looped you loop back and now you talk about the first ten You run your forest gun pattern the second ten you transition into the company as far as the company goes your third ten Right and with each as you go you end up further down the line So let's say your initial presentation you were right here when you ask you the second time you're now here you move for you move More forward on the line and also what does that mean? You've created more certainty. He's higher up on the certain scale now That makes sense. So I'm up when I run a loop. I always talk about all three tens Especially that first loop now when I run that first look like could also lower his action threshold It depends on what industry you're in Okay, but typically I don't try to lower it the first mile wait till now because many times I can do without He's ready now, but if he's not so let's say a loop ends then but I'll always ask for the order this time the second time now When I ask for the order for the second time that is the formal end to the loop The loop is now over the loop begins with an objection and I loop back I then Establish more certain to the three tens which brings me forward Further than when I start it and bam I asked for the order again and when I asked for the order Obeying the rules that over arcs the straight line energy and benefits out the right tone all the things I've taught you separately, right? I asked you and I shut up and I see what happens if he hops to a new objection What will I do I'm gonna say I I say why understand you feel this way? I'll answer his objection Is it I'll answer the objections you want to basically canned answers there so many I've given you already and they're in the script builder. They're also my post online for free, right? I'll answer those objections, but will I ask for the order again? No, I Would never just answer his objection and asked for the order Something has to go in between because what why because his objections a smoke stream for uncertainty or at this point It could also be just a really high action threshold So now what I'll answer his objection. I loop back again into presentation mode I have my tertiary presentation. I resell my product now I button up some emotional search for future pacing because maybe he's high up on Logical certainty, but he's not high enough enough on the scale for emotional certainty So I'll resell the product again future pace for emotion. I'll talk about myself again for emotion I'll talk about the company just but just a couple just a button up and then I will lower his action threshold I'm asking you what's the worst that can happen here? Let's say it doesn't work out See I'm wrong and when you get to the pattern right now because you have those patterns the boys now in this loop I'll lower his action threshold and After I lower the action threshold then I asked for the order again Now I've asked for the order for the third time and I shut up Very often this is where they buy you're gonna see tons of people buy here But just from every time you loop you hit the first 10 the second and the third 10 The first was mostly logic your second loop will be emotion more than logic And then the third one will be adding on action threshold And then you can also add on paint if you want or save that for the fourth loop because if after I lower his action So you know what they might say they don't give you objection. They'll say how much does that cost? They'll give you a buy signal How long will it take to get that? How long so no action question about the product? To show them that now because you're getting very close to buying they give you a buy signal when someone gives me a buy signal What do I do? I take a buy signal and turn that answer the question I'll loop back again very sure it's a little loop But not my certainty one more time say another and gray about the product great about myself becoming future paste them And I might add on some pain now say also by the way in terms of you feeling out of control about x yz I'll add on a bit of pain and then I'll ask for the order of fluff time Every time I ask for the order now foot. That's the end of that loop I'm always taking an objection or a buy signal and I use it as an opportunity to loop back on the line Into presentation mode. I increase certainty for the three tens and then depending on where I am in the process I might lower action threshold and up reduce add on pain and remember when you add on pain This is some fucking joy. I go here. I'm whatever that means. They're on pain. Okay when you add on pain It lowers the action threshold and there you have it That's what goes on when you loop the entire straight line is essentially one gigantic mechanisms one all in comp and all inclusive mechanism that allows you essentially to create absolute certainty in the mind and in the heart of your prospect absolute certainty without breaking rapport without pissing them off without doing something that by into itself Will simply stop the sale from closing you break a rule See what happens very often is by well how long maintenance so I just know get a sense here 40 minutes perfect, right? So I'm gonna take one sip of a red bull So what happens very often in the absence of running effective loops What people will typically do salesman will do is they violate a basic rule of Just of human contact of rapport building where and it just causes someone to Essentially fall out of rapport. But what does that really mean? Well, it's what does that mean? Let's say they fall out. Okay. We're so right. What does it mean? Guess what? It's a direct relation to which 10 the second 10 when you fall out of rapport with someone you are Blowing your second 10. They don't like you anymore. They don't try they don't connect with whatever the definition of rapport is rapport is this connection that we have that someone understands us someone feels our pain now Here now this is a good time. You know to really dig into this by way of Understand here's a really important over or you have all these overarching Concepts that sort of I said they sit atop or behind the straight line and govern all things in other words The straight line is like it's like the light switch to the bulb You have flick the switch the bulb goes on so you don't have to understand every little thing behind that process But you become proficient at pushing the switch and the bulb goes on you can do that because you've been trained to so you Don't have to understand all the deeper nuances of the straight line But when you do like we're doing now over time reinforcing it It just starts to make you that much more effective because when you really understand things your tonality Just naturally triggers and just makes you that much more effective. So let me explain something very very important to you about rapport, okay? And I've said this before but let me show you a different graph now, right? rapport is Not a constant It is not a constant. Let me just show you what does not happen. This is your open and This is your clothes. This is your open. This is your clothes You have your boundaries above and below the line right straight line You Have the first four seconds To take control of the conversation be perceived as an expert right got to do that why because without that They are in control. We've been taught to defer So we must because remember so this is another example There's so many these another overarching rule of the straight line another thing that just governs the straight line sits behind it on top It runs through it and make sure that if you don't abide by the rule You can't fucking close and that is is that we defer to people who we perceive to be experts People that we are in positions of authority of power. It's one of Chaldean ease drop Chaldean either professor who studied influence One of his princesses of basic fucking principle like I didn't invent that by the way I just know to abide by it. So you must be in control, but now let's talk about rapport rapport is not something you develop and Then bam, I got it. I'm in rapport like now. I'm good to go. I could be an asshole They get it. I'm but I'm in rapport rapport is Fleeting it goes up and down and so it goes based on the last word You said the last phrase that you uttered if they agree with it Rapport goes upside if they disagree report dips It's like when you watch women like watch one of these like a Political debates and you have two or three politicians on stage and they have the audience with 300 people They all have this little clicker in their hands, right? And if they when they agree with the politicians, they press their right thumb and when they disagree or when they're lying They press their left and they show this as like how they feel about the person and sure enough You watch when a politician is being honest and authentic and saying things that make sense Or all the right they click and you see that that thing go up and then they'll utter one stupid I'm now boom at nose dives and they're in the fucking red. They're out. They're like we don't trust them their assholes Same thing goes in a sale. You can't develop rapport with someone like okay. I admit it It is not easy and it's not fun to be below the wall. It's not so I most people are broke It's why most people never make it that far. It's a devil's trick Is that success comes after and you don't see it's invisible. You don't know so you give up That's why the world is filled people who have no money and yet some of us have figured that out Very few and figured out that if I keep going if I stay the course if I know I'm going if I know I'm making I just fucking believe and work really hard and execute the right strategies Success is the short That's why we're able to come back again and again No one can hold me down because I know the rules of success and I'm going to go through the shit to get there Are you? If you are You'll get there you will because you got me as your fucking coach Teaching you the rules success and making sure that you can leverage off my positivity until you got what it takes use me I have enough pot. I have enough belief for everybody on this video, but the whole world I do I know the truth I know because I've been there to hell and back guys All right I know that anyone can come back from failure and then anyone no matter where you grew up now what you know right now If you're going to put one foot in front of the other really embrace the system and work your fucking ass Oh, you can get whatever you want in life and you don't have to hurt anybody in the process You can help people along the way. That's the message for tonight I love you all and I'll see you again next there tonight for one more session before my surgery Then I'll have you take ones God bless and you'll receive an email with a new program. Let's rock and roll. Take care
